title: Liquid Ether
description: Three.js full Navier-Stokes fluid simulation with BFECC advection and an automated cursor-demo driver. Produces a premium swirling liquid ink effect as a full-bleed background.
prompt_text:
# Liquid Ether

Three.js full Navier-Stokes fluid simulation with BFECC advection and an automated cursor-demo driver. Produces a premium swirling liquid ink effect as a full-bleed background.

## Use when
- Creating a premium, fluid-ink atmosphere on a dark landing page or hero section.
- Adding a calming, hypnotic background that rewards cursor exploration.
- Showcasing a brand identity with a sophisticated, high-end animated background.

## Stack
- Framework: react
- Libraries: three

## Install
```bash
npm install three
```

## Component — liquid-ether.tsx
```tsx
// Source: animations_016.md
// Export: LiquidEther (default)
// Props: className, style, color (string), intensity (number), viscosity (number), autoDemo (boolean)
// Uses Three.js WebGLRenderTarget ping-pong for fluid simulation
// Implements full Navier-Stokes: advection (BFECC), divergence, pressure, gradient subtraction
// Automated demo driver runs pre-scripted cursor path when autoDemo=true
// Component is ~1087 lines. See full source in raw/animations_016.md
```

## Notes
- Component source is ~1087 lines. See `raw/animations_016.md` for full verbatim code.
- Requires Three.js (>600 KB). Lazy-load for performance.
- Most expensive simulation in this catalog. Test performance on target devices.
